2603.22869.title: Chain-of-Authorization: Internalizing Authorization into Large Language Models via Reasoning Trajectories
2603.22869.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become core cognitive components in modern artificial intelligence (AI) systems, combining internal knowledge with external context to perform complex tasks. However, LLMs typically treat all accessible data indiscriminately, lacking inherent awareness of knowledge ownership and access boundaries. This deficiency heightens risks of sensitive data leakage and adversarial manipulation, potentially enabling unauthorized system access and severe security crises. Existing protection strategies rely on rigid, uniform defense that prevent dynamic authorization. Structural isolation methods faces scalability bottlenecks, while prompt guidance methods struggle with fine-grained permissions distinctions. Here, we propose the Chain-of-Authorization (CoA) framework, a secure training and reasoning paradigm that internalizes authorization logic into LLMs' core capabilities. Unlike passive external defneses, CoA restructures the model's information flow: it embeds permission context at input and requires generating explicit authorization reasoning trajectory that includes resource review, identity resolution, and decision-making stages before final response. Through supervised fine-tuning on data covering various authorization status, CoA integrates policy execution with task responses, making authorization a causal prerequisite for substantive responses. Extensive evaluations show that CoA not only maintains comparable utility in authorized scenarios but also overcomes the cognitive confusion when permissions mismatches. It exhibits high rejection rates against various unauthorized and adversarial access. This mechanism leverages LLMs' reasoning capability to perform dynamic authorization, using natural language understanding as a proactive security mechanism for deploying reliable LLMs in modern AI systems.
2603.22869.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已成為現代人工智慧（AI）系統中的核心認知組件，將內部知識與外部上下文結合以執行複雜任務。<lb/>然而，LLMs 通常對所有可訪問數據不加區別地處理，缺乏對知識擁有權和訪問邊界的內在認識。<lb/>這一缺陷增加了敏感數據洩漏和對抗性操縱的風險，可能使未經授權的系統訪問和嚴重的安全危機成為可能。<lb/>現有的保護策略依賴於僵化的統一防禦，這阻礙了動態授權。<lb/>結構隔離方法面臨可擴展性瓶頸，而提示引導方法在細粒度許可區分方面則掙扎。<lb/>在此，我們提出了授權鏈（Chain-of-Authorization, CoA）框架，這是一種安全的訓練和推理範式，將授權邏輯內化為 LLMs 的核心能力。<lb/>與被動的外部防禦不同，CoA 重新構建了模型的信息流：它在輸入時嵌入許可上下文，並要求生成明確的授權推理軌跡，其中包括資源審查、身份解析和決策階段，然後才給出最終回應。<lb/>通過對涵蓋各種授權狀態的數據進行監督性微調，CoA 將政策執行與任務回應相結合，使授權成為實質性回應的因果前提。<lb/>廣泛的評估顯示，CoA 不僅在授權場景中保持了可比的效用，還克服了許可不匹配時的認知混淆。<lb/>它對各種未經授權和對抗性訪問顯示出高拒絕率。<lb/>這一機制利用 LLMs 的推理能力來執行動態授權，並使用自然語言理解作為在現代 AI 系統中部署可靠 LLMs 的主動安全機制。
2603.22869.author: Yang Li et.al.
2603.22869.authors: Yang Li, Yule Liu, Xinlei He, Youjian Zhao, Qi Li, Ke Xu
